criteria.label: State the tanh formula, output range, and value at zero; compute tanh on sample inputs.
criteria.example: Write tanh(x)=(e^x−e^(−x))/(e^x+e^(−x)). Range (−1,1), tanh(0)=0. Compute tanh(1)≈0.762 and tanh(−1)≈−0.762 showing odd symmetry.

criteria.label: Explain zero-centered output and why it differs from sigmoid activations.
criteria.example: Define f(0)=0 and f(−x)=−f(x). Contrast sigmoid outputs always in (0,1) centered at 0.5 vs tanh symmetric about zero for hidden-layer gradient bias.

criteria.label: Compare tanh vs sigmoid on range, center, max derivative, and typical layer roles.
criteria.example: Table: tanh (−1,1), center 0, max grad 1, RNN hidden; sigmoid (0,1), center 0.5, max grad 0.25, binary output and gates. Cite tanh(x)=2σ(2x)−1.

criteria.label: Derive or apply the tanh derivative and identify saturation-driven vanishing gradients.
criteria.example: Show d/dx tanh(x)=1−tanh²(x). For x=3, tanh(3)≈0.995, grad≈0.01 — explain why deep saturated tanh stacks learn slowly compared to ReLU.
